name: Mediterranean Diet
description: Classic Mediterranean recipes rich in olive oil, legumes, fresh vegetables, and seafood — one of the most researched healthy eating patterns in the world.
author: YOUR_NAME

recipes.title: Greek Salad with Feta
recipes.description: The authentic Horiatiki — chunky tomatoes, cucumbers, kalamata olives, and a thick slab of feta dressed with extra-virgin olive oil and oregano.

ingredients.name: ripe medium tomatoes, cut into chunks
large cucumber, cut into thick half-moons
flaky sea salt and black pepper

steps: Combine tomatoes, cucumber, green pepper, and red onion in a large bowl.
Add the olives and toss lightly.
Place the block of feta on top (do not crumble — authentic Greek salad has a whole piece).
Drizzle everything generously with olive oil.
Sprinkle oregano, sea salt, and black pepper over the top.
Serve immediately with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Hummus with Veggie Sticks
recipes.description: Smooth, restaurant-quality hummus made from scratch in 15 minutes. Served with a colourful platter of raw vegetables for dipping.

ingredients.name: extra-virgin olive oil, plus extra to serve

steps: Blend chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, and cumin in a food processor for 1 minute.
With the motor running, add the ice-cold water or chickpea liquid, one tablespoon at a time, until smooth and creamy.
Taste and adjust salt and lemon juice.
Spread into a shallow bowl, creating a swirl with the back of a spoon.
Drizzle with olive oil and dust with smoked paprika.
Serve with the vegetable sticks arranged around the bowl.

recipes.title: Baked Sea Bass with Lemon and Herbs
recipes.description: Whole sea bass baked on a bed of fennel and tomatoes, perfumed with lemon, garlic, and fresh herbs. Elegant and effortless.

ingredients.name: whole sea bass (approx. 400 g each), gutted and scaled

steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Scatter fennel and cherry tomatoes in a large baking dish. Drizzle with 2 tbsp olive oil and season.
Score each fish 3 times on each side with a sharp knife. Season inside and out with salt and pepper.
Stuff each fish cavity with lemon slices, garlic, thyme, and parsley.
Lay the fish on top of the vegetables. Drizzle with remaining olive oil and white wine.
Bake for 18–20 minutes until the flesh is opaque and flakes easily.
Serve straight from the dish with crusty bread to soak up the juices.

recipes.title: Tabbouleh
recipes.description: A refreshing Lebanese herb salad with fine bulgur wheat, mountains of parsley and mint, tomatoes, and a bright lemon dressing.

ingredients.name: flat-leaf parsley, very finely chopped (about 2 large bunches)

steps: Pour boiling water over the bulgur wheat, cover, and leave for 10 minutes to absorb. Fluff with a fork and let cool completely.
Finely chop the parsley and mint — they should make up the majority of this salad.
Combine parsley, mint, tomatoes, and spring onions in a large bowl.
Add the cooled bulgur and mix well.
Whisk olive oil and lemon juice together with a pinch of salt and pour over the salad.
Toss everything together. Taste and adjust lemon, salt, and pepper.
Allow to sit for 10 minutes before serving so the flavours meld.

recipes.title: Shakshuka
recipes.description: Eggs poached directly in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce — a classic one-pan breakfast or brunch.

ingredients.name: fresh parsley or coriander, to garnish
crusty bread or pitta, to serve

steps: Heat olive oil in a wide frying pan over medium heat. Add onion and pepper; cook for 7 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, cumin, paprika, coriander, and cayenne. Cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer for 8 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Make 4 wells in the sauce using the back of a spoon. Crack an egg into each well.
Cover the pan and cook for 5–7 minutes until the egg whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Scatter with feta (if using) and fresh herbs. Serve straight from the pan with bread.

recipes.title: Falafel Wrap
recipes.description: Crispy homemade falafel tucked into warm flatbreads with tahini sauce, crunchy vegetables, and pickled red cabbage.

ingredients.name: canned chickpeas, drained and well dried

steps: Pulse chickpeas, onion, garlic, parsley, coriander, cumin, flour, salt, and pepper in a food processor until coarse — not smooth. Refrigerate for 30 minutes if time allows.
Shape the mixture into 8–10 patties.
Heat olive oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Cook the falafels for 3–4 minutes per side until deep golden and crispy.
Mix tahini with lemon juice and 2 tbsp water to make a drizzleable sauce.
Warm the flatbreads in a dry pan or oven.
Spread tahini sauce on each flatbread, add cucumber, tomato, and pickled cabbage, then top with the hot falafels.
Fold or roll and serve immediately.
